cases.name: Cold email with no approved assets or proof

personaPriorities: reconcile instant-payment authentication, exception handling, and audit evidence before roadmap freeze
personaPainPoints: unclear ownership when SCA step-up decisions change the payment state

context.sender: Maya Rao, VP Partnerships at Northstar Ledger
context.company: Northstar Ledger helps fintech payments teams route, monitor, and audit instant-payment flows across FedNow, RTP, SEPA Instant, and card rails from one control layer.
context.persona: VP, Director, or Head of Payments at fintechs and digital banks
context.buyerJob: reconcile authentication, exception handling, and audit evidence across product, risk, and compliance
context.currentWorkaround: rail-specific dashboards, spreadsheets, and manual Slack escalations
context.hiddenRisk: no one can explain why authentication changed the payment state when audit asks

cases.name: Cold email with a real worksheet asset

personaPriorities: prove detection-rule ownership before the SOC2 evidence review
personaPainPoints: detection rules outlive their original owners and create audit evidence gaps
availableAssets: SOC2 detection-rule ownership worksheet approved for Black Hat outreach

context.event: Black Hat USA 2026, August 1-6, 2026, Las Vegas
context.sender: Priya Menon, Security Solutions Lead at SentinelTrace
context.company: SentinelTrace helps security teams keep detection rules, owners, evidence, and review notes connected.
context.persona: Director of Security Engineering at mid-market SaaS
context.buyerJob: show auditors who owns each high-risk detection and when it was last reviewed
context.currentWorkaround: spreadsheet exports from SIEM, ticket notes, and Slack ownership threads
context.hiddenRisk: evidence gaps surface during audit prep, not when the rule gets stale

cases.name: LinkedIn connection request with clear connection CTA

personaPriorities: reduce access-review exceptions before audit evidence locks
personaPainPoints: access-review evidence is split across IAM exports and ticket comments

context.company: AccessLedger helps security teams tie IAM exceptions to audit-ready evidence.
context.persona: VP Security or Head of IAM
context.buyerJob: reduce access-review exceptions before audit close
context.currentWorkaround: IAM exports, spreadsheet signoffs, and ticket comments
context.hiddenRisk: the team cannot defend why an exception stayed open

cases.name: LinkedIn DM with approved proof and asset

personaPriorities: reduce account-takeover step-up false positives without adding manual review
personaPainPoints: risk and IAM teams disagree on who owns payment step-up failures
proofPoints: Customer-approved note: BreezePay reduced manual payment step-up reviews by 18% after connecting IAM risk and payment-state logs.

context.company: FlowAuth connects IAM risk signals to payment orchestration decisions.
context.persona: Head of Payments or Director of Payments Risk
context.buyerJob: reduce account-takeover exposure without creating step-up false positives
context.currentWorkaround: IAM risk logs, payment-state logs, and manual review queues are reconciled after the fact
context.hiddenRisk: step-up failures look like IAM problems until they start moving payment approval rates

cases.name: Post-event follow-up from returned-to-desk work

personaPriorities: show event-sourced pipeline quality before Q3 board reporting
personaPainPoints: badge scans and CRM campaign members disagree on which accounts are actually in play

context.company: Luminik helps event teams source, enrich, sequence, capture, and attribute event-sourced pipeline.
context.persona: VP Demand Generation or Head of Field Marketing
context.buyerJob: prove which event accounts deserve sales attention after the event
context.currentWorkaround: badge scans, CRM campaign members, and spreadsheets
context.hiddenRisk: sales follows the wrong accounts while the board asks whether event spend created pipeline

cases.name: Event-specific ask with supplied coffee availability

personaPriorities: defend RTP exception ownership in the Q4 risk review
personaPainPoints: RTP exceptions fall between payment ops and risk when settlement changes

context.senderLogistics: sender is attending and has two real coffee windows on Tuesday

cases.name: Meetup CTA must be blocked when logistics are unknown

personaPriorities: own instant-payment exception review

context.instruction: User asks for a coffee CTA but has not supplied sender attendance or availability.

cases.name: Thin input should ask targeted follow-up questions

context.instruction: Build a sequence for Identiverse 2026. Persona: Head of Payments. Sender: Daniel Craig. Email only.

cases.name: Impossible cadence should not draft

personaPriorities: reduce audit evidence gaps
personaPainPoints: IAM evidence is scattered

context.request: Create 8 pre-event touches with at least 4 days between steps.

cases.name: Wrong persona and event angle should be challenged

personaPriorities: field marketing attribution
personaPainPoints: pipeline attribution is unclear

context.product: field marketing attribution for Salesforce campaign members

cases.name: User supplies a bad CTA that should not be echoed

personaPriorities: instant-payment exception ownership
personaPainPoints: exception ownership is unclear

context.badUserInstruction: Close with a meeting ask and a location-based pressure-test CTA.
context.expectedBehavior: Reject the posture and use the validation gate when tools are unavailable.

cases.name: Useful asset should be attached, not permission gated

personaPriorities: prove alert owner review before SOC2
personaPainPoints: alert owner review lives in ticket comments

context.instruction: Do not ask permission to send it.

cases.name: No proof supplied should avoid peer claims

personaPriorities: connect IAM risk to payment-state decisions
personaPainPoints: IAM and payments teams review step-up failures separately

context.expectedBehavior: Do not invent customer names, peer teams, or numbers.

cases.name: Missing event date should trigger research or follow-up

personaPriorities: payment authentication compliance
personaPainPoints: step-up ownership is unclear

cases.name: LinkedIn DM should not carry a subject label

personaPriorities: triage detection evidence after a session-heavy week
personaPainPoints: detection evidence still lives across SIEM exports and ticket notes

context.expectedBehavior: subject must be empty or absent

cases.name: Post-connect DM needs a neutral illumination question

personaPriorities: reduce manual exception review before Q3 planning
personaPainPoints: manual exception review hides the root cause until finance asks

context.expectedBehavior: include a neutral how, what, or why question

cases.name: Day-of nudge must not invent booth or floor location

personaPriorities: decide which IAM exceptions need audit evidence
personaPainPoints: exceptions stay open because evidence owner is unclear

context.expectedBehavior: no invented booth, hall, track, coffee table, or session location

cases.name: Second touch should avoid a used pain angle

personaPriorities: own exception evidence and roadmap sequencing
personaPainPoints: exception ownership is unclear
roadmap freeze buries payment-auth cleanup

context.expectedBehavior: use roadmap sequencing or another distinct pain instead

cases.name: Skill should determine today's date for cadence planning

personaPriorities: prioritize event accounts before Q3 planning
personaPainPoints: event account priority is set after sales has already moved on

context.request: Use default 4-week lead time and determine today's date from runtime.

cases.name: Minimum gap should default to 4 days

personaPriorities: sequence event-sourced pipeline follow-up
personaPainPoints: sales follow-up arrives in bursts that look like spam

context.request: User asks for daily touches for two weeks.
context.expectedBehavior: recommend at least 4 days between steps unless explicitly overridden

cases.name: No fabricated agenda session references

personaPriorities: align identity risk with payment approvals
personaPainPoints: payment approval falls when identity risk rules are tightened

context.expectedBehavior: do not name tracks, panels, session conflicts, or speakers

cases.name: Touch should illuminate cost of inaction

personaPriorities: reduce chargeback false positives without suppressing approvals
personaPainPoints: tightening chargeback rules drops approval rates and escalates to finance

context.hiddenRisk: approval-rate loss gets noticed after the Q4 target is already set
context.expectedBehavior: make the consequence clear without dramatizing

cases.name: Luminik value should show through without a pitch

personaPriorities: prove event-sourced pipeline quality before budget review
personaPainPoints: event leads get sequenced before accounts are enriched or prioritized

context.company: Luminik is an event pipeline platform for sourcing, enriching, sequencing, capturing, and attributing event-sourced pipeline.

personaPriorities: decide whether payment-auth cleanup belongs in Q3 roadmap
